text:
A long, long time ago on a quiet little wooded island, there was a giant owl whose head had turned into a large feathered hat. Owl always woke up feeling peculiar. Every day morning, he'd stretch his great big owl wings and glide softly through the forests. He loved to eat creepy-crawlies like moths—but there was something odd about the kind of moths he collected. They didn't buzz like ordinary moths.

One morning after a particularly unusual snacking session, Owl thought maybe there was something strange about the world. He buzzed through the forest.

Owl landed outside a little-known enchanted library. Books didn't move. They wouldn’t flutter in the breeze. Owl had seen oddities, but this was just weird.

He pushed the door open. "Could you please borrow a book?" Owl asked softly. The man behind the counter glanced up. His hair was balding, except for a tuft of white beard, and his spectacles perched on
